recent_candidates.excerpt: No Images? Click here https://fdd-new.cmail19.com/t/r-e-tutdjddy-oophjwr-e/ July 14, 2026 | This Is Beirut Erdogan Dreams of Annexing Arab Countries, Reviving the Turkish Empire [https://www.fdd.org/analysis/2026/07/14/erdogan-dreams-of-annexing-arab-countries-reviving-the-turkish-empire/] The prospect of a revived Turkish empire poses a new threat to Arab sovereignty. Hussain Abdul-Hussain [https://www.fdd.org/team/hussain-abdul-hussain/] | Research Fellow Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. (Utku Ucrak/Anadolu via Getty Images) When Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan presented Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam with a copy of his grandfather Salim Salam’s memoirs, he was making more than a diplomatic gesture. By invoking the elder Salam’s service in the Ottoman parliament, Erdogan cast himself as the legitimate heir to the Ottoman sultans and the Salams as subjects of the empire. This encounter was emblematic of a larger and more troubling ambition. Erdogan is determined to revive Turkish influence over Arab lands once occupied by the Ottomans. Turkey’s aspirations extend well beyond Lebanon. On June 7, Turkish Interior Minister Mustafa Ciftci openly said he hoped to one

recent_candidates.title: Young adults are poor despite every metric which suggests otherwise
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://becomingnoble.substack.com/p/young-adults-are-poor-despite-every A recent article from The Cut received a huge amount of attention: ‘It’s Hard to See My Parents Live So Lavishly While We’re Struggling’. [ https://substack.com/redirect/1e8e411a-6ea1-49b1-a74b-a7c75bed91eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] The piece includes vignettes of Millenial hardship: …his dad, Steve, sold his company to a private equity firm about five years ago, which enabled him to retire comfortably in his mid-60s… Two years ago, Joe asked his dad for a loan. He wanted to start a lawn-care business and needed capital for commercial-grade equipment and a trailer, at least $15,000. He presented Steve with his business plan, which included a schedule to pay back the money. Still, Steve said “no.”… Joe hasn’t started the business. He works for a property-management firm and does landscaping work on the side to bring in extra money. But he has two young kids of his own and finds it difficult to save. “Without some initial help, it’s pretty unrealistic that I’ll ever be able to go the entrepreneurial route,” he says. recent_candidates.title: An Introduction to Richard Walther Darré
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://antelopehillpublishing.substack.com/p/an-introduction-to-richard-walther This is an article version of a thread we did on Twitter/X, which you can find here [ https://substack.com/redirect/782a5b35-982e-4398-8c83-f96cbc4b21a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ]. This information is presented for purposes of historical education. Richard Walther Darré was a famous theorist of the concept of “Blood and Soil” in National Socialist Germany, who would become a high-ranking member of Hitler’s government. We have published Darré’s two books in English for the first time: A New Nobility of Blood and Soil [ https://substack.com/redirect/4529906e-71a0-40c5-8335-ff6c0aa833f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] The Peasantry as the Lifeblood of the Nordic Race [ https://substack.com/redirect/7c9027b5-3eb4-4338-ae1c-35b3cd4e3000?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] Darré was born July 14, 1895 to a German family in Buenos Aires, and sent to school in Germany as a child. recent_candidates.title: Take an epic journey with our new Odyssey puzzle, toast the 21st amendment, and find more gifts for history buffs from The Reader’s Catalog
recent_candidates.excerpt: “Tell me the tale of a man, Muse, who had so many roundabout ways To wander, driven off course, after sacking Troy’s hallowed keep...” —The Odyssey by Homer, as translated by Daniel Mendelsohn The World of The Odyssey & Other Greek Myths: 1,000-Piece Puzzle This 1,000-piece puzzle shows the saga of Odysseus’s famed journey in one epic scene. Discover the heroes, gods, and monsters—from the the king of Ithaca and the Acheans to Athena, Poseidon, and Hermes, to Scylla and Charybdis, the Minotaur, and much more—while you piece together this beautiful image and help Odysseus return to Telemachus and Penelope. Shop Now [https://shop.nybooks.com/products/the-world-of-the-odyssey-and-other-greek-myths?_pos=2&_psq=odyssey&_ss=e&_v=1.0] The Real Women of Greek Myths: 1,000-Piece Puzzle In this beautifully detailed jigsaw puzzle, rediscover the lives and stories of the women of Greek myths, from Medusa and Medea to Helen, Eurydice, Aphrodite, Phaedra, Artemis, and others. As you build the puzzle, look for Pandora and her jar, Medusa with snakes for hair, and other characters with their signifiers and relevant details. recent_candidates.title: Will Global Warming Solve the Fertility Crisis?
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.stevesailer.net/p/will-global-warming-solve-the-fertility From the Australian Broadcasting Company: Climate change becoming a major driver of child marriage across Asia and the Pacific [ https://substack.com/redirect/3362c13f-a67e-46cb-9c27-85b7fd8b7674?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] What can’t climate change do? It’s a floor wax and a desert topping! By Claire Campbell Thursday 9 July In short: The growing intensity of natural disasters across Asia is leading to increasing numbers of child marriages of girls, according to aid organisations. They’re worried this year’s El Niño could exacerbate the issue of child marriage. What’s next? Advocates are calling for funding and resources around child marriage to be better integrated into climate change response and resilience programs. Runa was 15 when she married a man she had never met. Cyclone Remal had ripped through her camp in Bangladesh’s Cox’s Bazar region, killing her family’s chickens and ducks, which were the source of both their food and income. Click here https://fdd-new.cmail19.com/t/r-e-tutdjljl-oophjwr-it/ July 14, 2026 | Policy Brief Kazakhstan Bets on Iranian Port Investment [https://www.fdd.org/analysis/2026/07/14/kazakhstan-bets-on-iranian-port-investment/] Angela Howard [https://www.fdd.org/team/angela-howard/] | CEFP Research Analyst Antonia-Laura Pup | CEFP Intern Shahid Rajaee Port. (via Wikimedia) Landlocked Kazakhstan is seeking port access, and Astana is banking on Iran to provide it. On July 8, Kazakhstan signed an agreement [https://astanatimes.com/2026/07/kazakhstan-iran-sign-deal-to-build-logistics-terminal-at-shahid-rajaee-port/?utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email] to build a logistics and transport terminal at Iran’s largest seaport [https://www.gov.kz/memleket/entities/mfa-tehran/press/news/details/1249333] and sanctions evasion hub [https://www.unitedagainstnucleariran.com/irans-threat-to-maritime-security], Shahid Rajee Port in Bandar Abbas. Under the terms of the 27-year Build-Operate-Transfer agreement [https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/botcontract.asp], Kazakhstan will construct the terminal in two years, operate it for 25, and then turn control over to Iran. recent_candidates.title: From convocation
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.pillarcatholic.com/p/from-convocation Hey everybody, Today’s the feast of St. Kateri Tekakwitha, and you’re reading The Tuesday Pillar Post. Well, sort of. I was out of the office yesterday, and I’m out still today, because I’m giving some talks at a convocation of priests from two dioceses in the Upper Midwest. I had the opportunity last night to spend time with many of those priests as they held a tournament, playing a complex card game which was brought to the United States by German immigrants in the last years of the 1800s, and is kept alive on the snow-swept prairies of our northern national borders. I did not enter the tournament, because I don’t know how to play, and a high-stakes presbyteral card night hardly seems the place to learn, especially since it’s a partner game, and I couldn’t ask anyone to sacrifice their run through the tournament to take a newbie as partner. Instead, I enjoyed drinks and conviviality with many of these men, and I was reminded how blessed we are in this country by the sort of priests who think nothing of routinely driving three hours to offer Mass for disparate communities — or, if they do think somethin

Mystery Grove Book Club Meeting 2: Always with Honor Chapters 4-6 Discussion
 Post
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.theconundrumcluster.com/p/mystery-grove-book-club-meeting-2 Hello everyone, I’m very happy to report that our first book club episode [ https://substack.com/redirect/98ae1fe2-375f-4787-ae23-6c7830600ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] was a huge success! One (paid) subscriber said “This is the greatest episode of MGs entire catalogue.” There is still a very lively dialogue ongoing in the comments section [ https://substack.com/redirect/98ae1fe2-375f-4787-ae23-6c7830600ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ], I really am grateful for all the hard work everyone is putting in on this. This week we’re moving on to the next three chapters of the book, which cover the White Army’s military high point during the Russian Civil War. This has been a lot of fun so far and I’m confident that will continue. It’s a lot more rewarding to read a book alongside a bunch of other people. If you haven’t started A;ways with Honor yet, there’s still time to catch up. You can order the brand-new edition (with new maps and appendices) here [ https://substack.com/redirect/035559b6-c6db-49cb-b846-4a5ee

recent_candidates.title: Dead Weight Center
recent_candidates.excerpt: Sponsored by Bloomsbury Academic [https://www.bloomsbury.com/us/] For the Review’s July 23 issue, Bryce Covert reads Dead Center [https://www.nybooks.com/articles/2026/07/23/compromised-values-dead-center-joe-manchin/], the unsettlingly titled memoir by former senator and former Democrat Joe Manchin. As Covert writes, the adamant centrist and underminer of his former party’s legislative efforts has written a similarly frustrating book: It feels as if he’s speaking from an alternate universe, one where the structures of our political system aren’t being torn down and our neighbors aren’t being terrorized before our eyes by authoritarian violence. His book may have nothing to say about the very real dangers we face, but it is starkly revealing of the worldview that got us here. And part of that worldview? An uncompromising insistence on compromising Democratic priorities: Manchin sees power as something to be used as a last resort, if at all.… This, to him, is somehow unthinkable—that a party elected on commitments to increase the minimum wage and send out stimulus checks would use the authority voters had handed it to actually do those things. Below, alongside Covert’s essay, we hav

recent_candidates.title: Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center
recent_candidates.excerpt: Earlier this spring, Claude’s developers found the cyber potential of Anthropic’s model, Mythos 5, hazardous enough to pump the brakes on model release, creating the Project Glasswing collaboration to give cyber defenders the upper hand through controlled access to the model. Despite these efforts, the U.S. government stepped in on June 12, 2026, and forced Anthropic to take down Mythos and its safeguarded form, Fable 5 — an action the company disputed, calling the trigger a narrow, nonuniversal jailbreak. A lack of standard processes, complex legal terrain, and information-sharing obstacles resulted in a “pickup game” where, absent a shared factual The post Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center appeared first on War on the Rocks .

recent_candidates.title: Breaking America’s Promise to Pacific Island Veterans
recent_candidates.excerpt: In January, Robson Henry died at the age of 66. Chances are, you have never heard of Henry. He was not famous, rich, or powerful. In many ways, Henry was no different from the thousands of young men and women who, at the age of 18, chose to serve by enlisting in the U.S. Army. Nor does the fact that he stayed in the Army for the next 26 years, serving multiple tours of duty in the Middle East, meaningfully distinguish him. And yet, Henry was special: Unlike the vast majority of his fellow soldiers, he was not from the The post Breaking America’s Promise to Pacific Island Veterans appeared first on War on the Rocks .

recent_candidates.title: NATO’s Unity on Paper and Fractures Beneath the Surface
recent_candidates.excerpt: On July 7-8, NATO held its 36th official summit in Ankara, Turkey. There were several consequential topics to discuss at this year’s meeting, chief among them the wars in Ukraine and Iran and the uncertain future of European defense and security. We asked five experts to assess the outcomes of the summit for the various parties involved.Liviu HorovitzAssociate Researcher at German Institute for Security and International AffairsThe key takeaway from Ankara is that even President Donald Trump is struggling to escape NATO’s strategic logic. recent_candidates.title: Why Are Only 3 of 777 Stuyvesant High Freshmen Black?
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.stevesailer.net/p/why-are-only-3-of-777-stuyvesant From the New York Times news section: An Elite N.Y.C. Public School Admitted 777 Students. Only 3 Were Black. [ https://substack.com/redirect/94b03b6e-e6b2-4767-a8d9-1e440709476d?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] New York’s specialized high schools give disproportionately few seats to Black and Hispanic students, continuing a pattern that has drawn criticism. By Matthew Haag July 14, 2026 A disproportionately small number of Black and Hispanic students received admission offers to New York City’s elite public high schools for the upcoming academic year, continuing a pattern of racial and ethnic gaps that has existed for years despite promises by elected officials to address the divide. You might also point out that not only has this disparity existed for decades in New York City, it exists all over the rest of the country too. Famous public exam schools like Thomas Jefferson in the D.C. suburbs and Lowell in San Francisco have the exact same problems getting equal percentages of each race to pass the entrance test. recent_candidates.title: Breasterton’s Fence
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.raweggstack.com/p/breastertons-fence “Don’t ever take a fence down until you know why it was put up.” That’s a rather more concise way of expressing “Chesterton’s Fence”: a principle that urges us to exercise caution before we sweep away institutions, practices, buildings and structures without fully understanding why they came to exist in the first place. The fence is a homely example, befitting the great fat man himself, but real-world instances have been devastating and tragic and anything but quaint. Take China’s “Four Pests” campaign, begun under Chairman Mao in the late 1950s. In the name of maximising agricultural output, farmers were encouraged to massacre sparrows wherever they were found, since they eat grain. Less grain, lower yields—no Great Leap Forward. But when the sparrow population collapsed as intended, insects like locusts ravaged the crops. Famine ensued. Millions died. The humble sparrow, it turned out, played an important role in keeping insects and other pests in check, a role that more than balanced the annoyance of a bit of lost grain. These days, if I reach for an example to illustrate Chesterton’s fence, I might re

recent_candidates.title: Short Problem 10
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://tacticalnotebook.substack.com/p/short-problem-10 The following problem appeared in the first semi-annual volume of the Infantry School Mailing List, which rolled off the press in 1931. (This periodical, which might well be described as a journal in all but name, replaced the collections of mimeographed instructional materials that the US Army Infantry School sent out to subscribers in the 1920s.) For the philosophy behind theses problems, please see: PROBLEM NO. 10 ‘All delays are dangerous in war.’ Dryden Tyrannic Love SITUATION Blues are attacking northward. The right assault company of a battalion, with two platoons in assault, is disposed as indicated [on the map]. A large quarry with vertical sides has been discovered in front of the left assault platoon. A patrol has found the woods free of the enemy. Some fire is coming from B. Ground in front of the company is slightly rolling and covered with scattered underbrush. One section of [two] machine guns is firing from between the two assault platoons. The position of other machine guns or the battalion reserve is not known. The company on the left has just taken the hill at C and is reorganiz

recent_candidates.title: The Company and the Quarry
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://tacticalnotebook.substack.com/p/the-company-and-the-quarry This coming Saturday (18 July 2026), participants in the two Zoomdezvous of the Decision Game Club will take on the of a captain of American infantry who, in 1931 or so, finds himself attacking over the same piece of eccentric terrain in three slightly different time lines. I invite all those who have taken out paid subscriptions to one of my newsletters - whether The Tactical Notebook or Extra Muros - to join in the fun. (Folks serving in the armed forces of countries that I like are also welcome to participate.) If you are already on the distro list, you will already have received the weekly announcement that, in addition to telling you a little about the game, provides log in information. If you would like to joint the distro list, please send an e-mail message to thecasemethodclub@gmail.com. We will meet twice this Saturday, once at noon and once at eight pm. Please note that all times are those of the great metropolises of the east coast of North America - Montréal, New York, and Quantico. Related Reading Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly90YWN0aWNhbG5vdG

[https://www.fdd.org/?p=299720] Vis-à-vis Moscow and Tehran, Trump should do the opposite Clifford D. May [https://www.fdd.org/team/clifford-d-may/] | Founder & President (Morteza Nikoubazl/NurPhoto via Getty Images) President Joe Biden tried to talk wars down. For example, on Jan. 19, 2022, he suggested – hopefully, it seemed to me – that Russia’s coming invasion of Ukraine would be only a “minor incursion.” No such luck. Just over a month later, President Vladimir Putin’s tanks rolled toward Kyiv on a mission to drag Ukraine back under the Kremlin jackboot. Similarly, Mr. Biden offered Iran’s rulers sanctions relief and diplomatic engagement even as they enriched uranium, stockpiling enough by 2025 for multiple nuclear weapons. The theocrats were not interested in rapprochement. For 47 years they’ve stated their goals with crystalline clarity: “Death to America!” [https://www.memri.org/tv/iran-supreme-leader-ayatollah-ali-khamenei-death-america-not-slogan-policy-west-protests] and “Death to Israel!” The latter is meant literally: the extermina

recent_candidates.title: Enter Through the Gift Shop
recent_candidates.excerpt: Art en passant Change is afoot at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York. The usual fanfare around the Met Gala, which every May inaugurates an exhibition under the auspices of the museum’s Costume Institute, was heightened this year with the opening of the Condé M. Nast Galleries, a new space just off the museum’s main entrance. The first show in the Nast Galleries, “Costume Art,” makes an argument familiar to regular attendees of the institute’s exhibitions: that, as the institute’s curator Andrew Bolton writes in the catalog, “the history of aesthetics cannot be disentangled from the history of the body that clothes it.” In “Fashion Forward,” from our July 23, 2026, issue, Jed Perl challenges this line of thinking [https://www.nybooks.com/articles/2026/07/23/fashion-forward-costume-institute-moma/], and in the process raises important questions about what these prominent new galleries portend for the country’s most important encyclopedic art museum. “What’s troubling,” he writes, “isn’t that there are fashion exhibitions at the Met. What’s troubling is that there is reason to believe that the fashion industry has bought what amounts to prime real estate at the Met—and bought

recent_candidates.title: Reading through the Lines of the FY2027 NDAA
recent_candidates.excerpt: The relationship between Congress and the Pentagon over the past year has been marked by public disagreements and frustrations over lawmakers’ concerns about the Iran War, and by what some lawmakers view as politically motivated firings of top military leaders. In many ways, the House and Senate versions of the Fiscal Year 2027 National Defense Authorization Act reflect lawmakers’ efforts to check the Pentagon’s policy power.Innovation efforts are generally a bright spot in these political battles. While the reforms proposed this year across the chambers were more modest than last year’s transformational SPEED and FoRGED Acts, lawmakers continue to advance The post Reading through the Lines of the FY2027 NDAA appeared first on War on the Rocks .

recent_candidates.title: The Peace That Redistributes War: What Camp David Reveals About Lebanon and Iran
recent_candidates.excerpt: Nothing reveals truths more effectively than patterns. If you can identify a pattern, you can see what actions conceal, what statements leave unsaid, and what agreements postpone. Peace treaties and the negotiations surrounding them are rarely analyzed in this way, even though they may exhibit patterns that recur across different circumstances. Consequently, there is often a simplification in understanding the pressures, incentives, and constraints shaping each step and each party’s choices, an exaggeration in linking the various warring factions into a single understanding without seeing the gaps, or a disregard for realities that might prevent us from truly understanding the The post The Peace That Redistributes War: What Camp David Reveals About Lebanon and Iran appeared first on War on the Rocks .
